home.hero.label: Embedded delivery consulting · Agile + AI without theater
home.hero.lead: I work hands-on with software teams that need to adapt faster without turning delivery into chaos: better feedback loops, clearer technical decisions, and AI leverage grounded in real work.
home.stats.years.label: Years of Experience
home.stats.engagements.label: Client Engagements
home.stats.companies.label: Companies
home.expertise.heading: A senior operator inside the delivery system.
home.expertise.1.title: Embedded delivery, not slideware
home.expertise.1.description: I get close enough to feel the bottlenecks with the team, then improve the workflow where the work actually happens.
home.expertise.2.title: Decision quality under pressure
home.expertise.2.description: Architecture, ownership, trade-offs, and escalation paths become explicit so teams can move without guessing.
home.expertise.3.title: AI with judgment loops
home.expertise.3.description: Agents, personas, and workflow automation are useful when the team can steer them with context, review discipline, and clear boundaries.
home.philosophy.heading: No consulting theater. No AI dogma. No commodity staff augmentation.
home.philosophy.p1: Useful change comes from inside the work. Detached recommendations often sound clean and fail on contact with organizational reality.
home.philosophy.p2: The goal is not to install a framework. It is to help a team learn faster, decide clearer, and ship safer while keeping technical credibility in the loop.
home.philosophy.p3: AI belongs in that picture, but only as leverage. When the system is vague, brittle, or politically unclear, AI amplifies the mess. When the system is coherent, it compounds the team's effectiveness.
home.philosophy.quote: Hands-on enough to feel the bottlenecks, senior enough to fix them.
home.cta.heading: If your team is being asked to move faster with AI, but the delivery system is already noisy, we should talk.
home.cta.lead: I work selectively with engineering leaders and change sponsors who want practical improvement from inside real delivery — not another deck about transformation.
home.profile.heading: Other channels
home.profile.lead: Low-noise places to verify the human behind the terminal.

work.img.alt: Profile picture of Martin Trenker, independent software consultant
work.title: Martin Trenker
work.subtitle: Independent Software Consultant
work.about.heading: About me
work.about.p1: Across 19 engagements, from global enterprises to smaller product teams, the pattern was consistent: when architecture is brittle and technical decisions stay implicit, delivery slows down regardless of stack.
work.about.p2: That is the work I call Progressive Engineering. I help teams strengthen architecture, improve technical decision quality, and communicate clearly enough to move faster without increasing risk.
work.contact.heading: Contact
work.skills.heading: Skills
work.skills.programming.heading: Programming
work.skills.org.heading: Organizational
work.section.cleanAgile.heading: Architecture
work.section.cleanAgile.p: Architecture is where change either gets easier or more expensive. I work on boundaries, coupling, and system shape so teams can move faster without recurring structural drag.
work.section.learner.heading: Communication
work.section.learner.p: Most expensive technical problems are half-technical and half-communicative. I make ownership, constraints, and trade-offs explicit so teams can decide earlier and escalate less.
work.section.automation.heading: AI Leverage
work.section.automation.p: AI becomes useful when a team can steer it with judgment. I use agents as force multipliers for well-structured work and help teams build the habits that keep speed from turning into noise.
work.projects.heading: Projects
work.spotlight.heading: Selected Work
work.spotlight.lead: Selected engagements where architecture, communication, and technical leadership reduced brittleness and improved changeability.

contact.context.heading: What to expect
contact.context.response: I respond to every message personally, usually within one business day.
contact.context.process: A first step is typically a short call to understand your situation and decide whether there is a good fit.
contact.context.selectivity: I work with a small number of clients at any time, so I am selective about engagements — that selectivity is what keeps the work effective.

privacy.metadata.description: Privacy policy and information about the processing of personal data on clean.dev according to the GDPR.
privacy.controller.heading: Controller
privacy.analytics.heading: Web analytics (Plausible Analytics)
privacy.analytics.p1: This website uses Plausible Analytics, a privacy-friendly analytics tool. Plausible does not set cookies and does not store personal data.
privacy.analytics.p2: Anonymous statistics are collected, such as page views, country of origin, and browser type. The data is processed on an own server and is not shared with third parties.
privacy.analytics.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in anonymous usage statistics without cross-site tracking).
privacy.contactForm.heading: Contact form
privacy.contactForm.p1.afterLink: , your details (name, email address, message content) are processed to handle your enquiry.
privacy.contactForm.p2: The data is transmitted by email to the controller and is not stored in a database. Your IP address is processed temporarily in server memory for spam protection and is not persisted.
privacy.contactForm.recipientValue: Email inbox of the controller. The email delivery provider {provider} acts as a processor according to Art. 28 GDPR.
privacy.contactForm.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. b GDPR (pre-contractual measures at the request of the data subject).
privacy.contactForm.retentionValue: Until your enquiry has been fully processed.
privacy.hosting.heading: Hosting & server logs
privacy.hosting.p2: Whenever a page is accessed, the server log automatically stores the following data: IP address, date and time, requested URL, HTTP status code, amount of data transferred, browser, and operating system.
privacy.hosting.p3: This data is required for the secure operation of the website and is deleted automatically after {retention}.
privacy.hosting.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest: security and abuse prevention).
privacy.fonts.heading: Google Fonts
privacy.fonts.p1: This website uses Google Fonts (Google LLC, 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA) to display fonts. When the fonts are loaded, your IP address is transmitted to Google servers.
privacy.fonts.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in a consistent website appearance).
privacy.fonts.moreInfo: Further information can be found in Google's privacy policy

review.error.expired.body: For security reasons, review links are time-limited. Request a fresh link to continue.

review.form.linkedin.title: Continue with LinkedIn
review.form.linkedin.body: Connecting with LinkedIn attaches your verified display name and LinkedIn profile identifier to the submission, making reviewer identity easier to confirm. LinkedIn will share your name, email address, and profile ID with this site.
review.form.linkedin.connected: You are signed in with LinkedIn. Your LinkedIn identity will be attached to this submission.
review.form.linkedin.secondaryLink: Use the manual form below to submit without it.
review.form.name: Name
review.form.feedback.placeholder: What changed through the collaboration? Please include outcomes and context.
review.form.consent: I confirm my name and the feedback I have written may be forwarded to the site owner and published as a testimonial on clean.dev.
review.form.consent.privacyNote: Your data (name, email, role, and feedback) is forwarded to the site owner by email and is not stored in a database. See <privacyLink>Privacy</privacyLink> for details.
review.form.privacyHint: No data is sent before you click the button above.
review.form.success.title: Review submitted successfully
review.form.success.body: Thank you for your feedback. It has been forwarded and you will be contacted if needed.
review.form.error.submission: Something went wrong while submitting your review. Please try again.
review.form.error.tokenExpired: This review link has expired. Please request a new invitation link.
review.form.error.tokenInvalid: This review link is no longer valid. Please request a new invitation link.
review.form.error.emailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
review.form.error.feedbackLength: Please provide at least {min} characters of feedback.
privacy.reviewFlow.heading: Review flow (LinkedIn sign-in and feedback form)
privacy.reviewFlow.p1: When you follow a personal review invitation link to clean.dev, you may optionally sign in with LinkedIn via OAuth 2.0. LinkedIn will share your display name, email address, and a unique LinkedIn profile identifier with this application. LinkedIn session data is not written to the application database.
privacy.reviewFlow.p2: Whether or not you use LinkedIn, you fill in a form with your name, email address, an optional role, your relationship to the project, and your written feedback. Explicit consent is required before the form can be submitted.
privacy.reviewFlow.p3: On submission, all form data and any LinkedIn identity are forwarded immediately by email to the controller. Nothing is written to the application database.
privacy.reviewFlow.recipientValue: Email inbox of the controller. The email delivery provider {provider} acts as a processor according to Art. 28 GDPR.
privacy.reviewFlow.legalBasisValue: Art. 6 para. 1 lit. a GDPR (your explicit consent to testimonial use of your name and feedback); Art. 6 para. 1 lit. f GDPR (legitimate interest in confirming reviewer identity via LinkedIn).
privacy.reviewFlow.retentionValue: Submission data is forwarded immediately and is not retained by the application. The controller's email inbox retains the received message subject to standard email retention practices.
privacy.reviewFlow.linkedinNote: LinkedIn identity verification is handled by LinkedIn Ireland Unlimited Company, Wilton Plaza, Wilton Place, Dublin 2, Ireland. LinkedIn processes your data according to their own privacy policy.
home.terminal.output: Checking delivery friction, decision latency, brittle architecture, and AI-readiness. Recommendation: join the real workflow, fix the system from inside, keep humans in judgment loops.
home.terminal.01: AI does not fix broken process. It exposes vague ownership, slow feedback, and unclear interfaces faster.
home.terminal.02: Agile means learning speed and adaptive execution — not ceremony theater or transformation theater.
home.terminal.03: Technical depth stays in the room so speed does not quietly become production risk.
home.expertise.lead: The work combines team-level process, architecture, and AI-enabled workflows because those problems do not fail in separate boxes.
home.proof.heading: Built for complex organizations, not demo-only narratives.
home.proof.case.label: current proof vector: Douglas / enterprise delivery friction
home.proof.case.body: The strongest current direction is persona-driven backlog shaping and workflow augmentation around tools teams already live in: Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps, and the messy reality around them.
